title: dipterocarpus retusus subsp. tonkinensis
intro: Dipterocarpus retusus subsp. tonkinensis, also known as the Tonkin dipterocarp, is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The Tonkin dipterocarp has been found to possess medicinal properties due to its rich content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and its extracts have been reported to exhibit antiviral and antimalarial activities, offering potential health benefits in the prevention and treatment of infectious diseases. Herbal preparations of Dipterocarpus retusus subsp. tonkinensis can be made in the form of infusions, decoctions, or extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and further research is needed to fully explore its medicinal and therapeutic potential.
